LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF NIMER A. MASSIS'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE TO DEFENDANTS RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF CANNOT PREVAIL ON THE MERITS OF HIS CLAIM BASED ON CC 2923.5 BECAUSE THE SUBJECT PROPERTY IS NOT PLAINTIFF'S PRIMARY RESIDENCE. MOREOVER, THERE HAS NOT BEEN A SHOWING THAT THIS PROPERTY IS "UNIQUE" ENOUGH FOR PLAINTIFF TO SHOW IRREPARABLE HARM. FINALLY, PLAINTIFF CANNOT POST A SUFFICIENT BOND. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. (302/LMG) JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI; REPORTER: LORETTA NAJERA, CSR #5153
DISMISSAL OF ENTIRE ACTION OF ALL PARTIES AND ALL CAUSES OF ACTION WITH PREJUDICE

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - OFF CALENDAR, DISMISSAL OF ENTIRE ACTION FILED ON AUGUST 26, 2011. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

Plaintiff Nimer A. Massis' Order To Show Cause To Defendants Re: Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Aug-24-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF NIMER A. MASSIS'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE TO DEFENDANTS RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION.
No appearances. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff's request for a preliminary injunction is denied. Plaintiff cannot prevail on the merits of his claim based on CC 2923.5 because the subject property is not plaintiff's primary residence. Moreover, there has not been a showing that this property is "unique" enough for plaintiff to show irreparable harm. Finally, plaintiff cannot post a sufficient bond. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Reporter: Loretta Najera, CSR #5153
